text: Skim read the text in ONE minute and check your answers to exercise 1.
When asked their favourite animals, many people answer 'dolphins'. They are known as friendly, intelligent

explanation: به هر موجود زنده‌ای اعم از حیوان یا انسان اطلاق می‌شود؛ در متون آیلتس برای تنوع بخشیدن به واژه animals استفاده می‌شود.

text: that have a special relationship with humans. For example, dolphins can tell when a woman is pregnant. They can also tell when someone is in trouble. No one is really sure why this is the case. Experts think they may understand that humans are similar to them and try to protect them from

explanation: حیواناتی که حیوانات دیگر را برای غذا شکار می‌کنند. واژه‌ای کلیدی در موضوعات حیات وحش آیلتس.

text: and other dangers. Dolphins' protection of humans might not be just automatic or

explanation: رفتاری که بر اساس غریزه است و یاد گرفته نشده. متضاد واژه learned یا deliberate.

text: : they may actively decide to help in certain situations.
There are many stories about dolphins protecting humans from sharks. Wildlife filmmaker Hardy Jones was filming a group of dolphins, when a large shark swam towards him ready to attack, Four dolphins

explanation: یک عبارت کاربردی به معنای نجات دادن کسی در لحظه خطر.

text: and drove the shark away. Perhaps they could tell he was a person who cared very much about dolphins. In fact, Jones was a well-known

explanation: کسی که در فعالیت‌های اجتماعی یا سیاسی برای ایجاد تغییر شرکت می‌کند.

text: against the killing of dolphins. In another incident, in 2004, in New Zealand, four people were saved from a great white shark by a

explanation: واحد شمارش و اسم جمع مخصوص گروهی از دلفین‌ها یا نهنگ‌ها.
جمع کردن افراد یا حیوانات در یک گروه و حرکت دادن آن‌ها به سمتی خاص.

text: the swimmers into a group and formed a protective ring around them. As they had not yet seen the shark, one of them tried to swim away. He couldn't get away, because every time he tried, he was pushed back inside the ring by the dolphins.
Dolphins don't only save humans from sharks, but protect them in other situations too. A scuba diver was hit by a boat near the Channel Islands (between England and France) in 2006. The man was

explanation: حالتی که فرد به دلیل ضربه یا بیماری بیدار نیست و از محیط آگاه نیست.

text: , but survived 56 hours in the water, watched over by a pod of about 150 dolphins. In 2014, dolphins again came to the help of a human. Joey Trevino was losing hope. He had been in the sea for 24 hours after his boat sank in the Gulf of Mexico. He felt he couldn't keep going any more. A friendly dolphin approached him and gently pushed him, as if to say 'don't give up'. That

explanation: دادن تشویق و اعتماد به نفس به کسی به جای کمک مادی.

text: and encouragement may have saved Trevino's life.
Dolphins have also been known to help other

explanation: طبقه‌ای از حیوانات یا گیاهان که ویژگی‌های مشابهی دارند. واژه بسیار پر تکرار در لیسنینگ و ریدینگ آیلتس.

text: . In New Zealand, two pygmy sperm whales were in difficulty next to a

explanation: ناحیه‌ای کم‌عمق در دریا یا رودخانه که به دلیل تجمع شن ایجاد شده است.

text: . People were trying their best to get them back out to sea, but the whales couldn't find their way past the sand bank. After several hours of failed attempts, they were ready to give up. Along came 'Moko', a bottlenose dolphin, who seemed to communicate with the whales and led them to a channel which took them back to the ocean.
Whales have also been known to protect both humans and other

explanation: حیواناتی که به نوزادان خود شیر می‌دهند.

text: . In California, in 2012, a BBC Planet Earth film crew filmed a group of humpback whales who were protecting

explanation: حرکت فصلی حیوانات از یک منطقه به منطقه دیگر.

text: grey whales from attacks by orcas (killer whales) over a period of at least seven hours. In China, Yang Yun applied for a job training whales at Polar Land in the city of Harbin. For the 'Interview' she had to dive down as far as possible in the seven-metre deep pool. When she was about five metres down. Yang Yun found she couldn't move her legs due to the freezing temperatures. Two beluga whales, Mila and Nicola,

explanation: درک کردن چیزی بدون اینکه مستقیماً گفته شود یا دیده شود.

text: that she was in trouble. Mila

explanation: گرفتن چیزی به صورت محکم و ناگهانی.

text: Yang Yun's leg in her mouth and pushed Yun to the surface, saving her life.
Stories of marine mammals helping humans and each other

explanation: برای بیان پیشینه تاریخی یک موضوع استفاده می‌شود.

text: Ancient Greece. Although we may never be sure why they help us, many people feel that it is a good reason for us to do whatever we can to protect them.
